description_long: This is a small, black stringed instrument with a compact, rounded body and a narrow neck, resembling a soprano ukulele or a parlor guitar. The body is finished in a glossy black color, with a wooden bridge and four visible strings stretching from the bridge to the headstock. The neck appears to be made of wood, possibly with a painted or stained finish, and the fretboard is marked with metallic frets. The headstock features four tuning pegs, two on each side, and lacks any visible branding or logo. The back of the neck and body are also black, with the neck showing a subtle blueish tint, likely from lighting or paint.
description: A small, glossy black stringed instrument with a rounded body, wooden neck, and four strings, resembling a ukulele or parlor guitar. It features a simple headstock with four tuning pegs and a wooden bridge.
description_view_0: Front view shows the glossy black body, wooden bridge, four strings, and the full fretboard leading to the headstock with four tuning pegs.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the instrument's slim profile, the thickness of the body, and the curvature of the neck.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the black body and neck, with the headstock and tuning pegs visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the slim body and neck, with the back curvature and thickness of the instrument.
attribute_headstock_design: Simple, rounded headstock with four tuning pegs (two per side), no visible logo or distinctive cutouts
